specs.title: at-matrix
specs.summary: This matrix records assistive-technology (AT) test results for the representative component set defined in `at-methodology.md`. Testing was performed manually; stacks not available in the current environment are marked `not tested`. Results are point-in-time — re-run when components change.

specs.title: at-methodology
specs.summary: This document defines cascade's assistive-technology (AT) testing methodology.

specs.title: brand-color
specs.summary: | Token                      | oklch value             | Approx hex | Usage                                                                                                                            | | -------------------------- | ----------------------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `--cascivo-brand-primary`  | `oklch(0.55 0.15 240)`  | `#0079bf`  | Hero bg, CTA button, logo gradient start                                                                                         | | `--cascivo-brand-accent`   | `oklch(0.72 0.13 195)`  | `#00bdbe`  | Gradient end, accent UI, flow highlights                                                                                         | | `--cascivo-brand-ink`      | `oklch(0.22 0.03 250)`  | `#101c28`  | Wordmark on light, body text on brand surfaces                                                                                   | | `--cascivo-brand-paper`    | `oklch(0.99 0.005 250)` | `#f9fcff`  | Brand surface (hero, OG card background)                                                                                         | | `--cascivo-brand-gradient` | —                       | —          | `linear-gradient(135deg, var(--cascivo-brand-primary), var(--cascivo-brand-accent))` — hero fill, logo gradient variant, OG card |

specs.title: cascivo-launch-checklist
specs.summary: Code prerequisites completed in v15 T1–T4. External steps the owner must perform:

specs.title: chart-palette
specs.summary: The `--cascivo-chart-1..8` tokens are grounded in the **Okabe-Ito 8-color palette**, the de-facto standard for scientific figures requiring color vision deficiency (CVD) safety.

specs.title: Color Usage
specs.summary: cascade uses a three-level token system for color. AI agents and component authors must use semantic tokens, never primitive color values.

specs.title: Content Tone
specs.summary: cascade components with user-visible chrome text (labels, aria strings, empty states) follow these voice guidelines.

specs.title: Before/After Context Impact Demo
specs.summary: Reproducible comparison: each prompt run twice.

specs.title: Decision Hierarchy
specs.summary: When guidance conflicts, resolve it in this order:

specs.title: legal-mapping
specs.summary: Cascade targets WCAG 2.2 AA conformance at the component level. All three major accessibility frameworks — EAA, EN 301 549, and US Section 508 — reference WCAG as their technical standard.

specs.title: parity-matrix
specs.summary: **Last updated:** 2026-06-14 **Generated artifact:** `apps/docs/public/parity.json` (via `scripts/parity/generate.ts` — do NOT hand-edit the JSON)

specs.title: perf-methodology
specs.summary: This document diagnoses the current state of the per-component incremental bundle matrix before any fixes land (T3-T2 and later). Read `apps/bench/METHODOLOGY.md` for the overall benchmark methodology; this doc focuses specifically on the incremental matrix and the two known defects that corrupt its output.

specs.title: shadcn-comparison-2026-07
specs.summary: **Status:** analysis + plan only — nothing in this document is implemented yet. **Scope:** landing page (cascivo.com), documentation (docs.cascivo.com + `docs/`), GitHub repo presentation. **Benchmark:** shadcn/ui as of July 2026 (the most popular UI library; ~118k stars, ~4M+ weekly CLI downloads). **Method:** four parallel deep audits (landing code, docs surface, repo hygiene, shadcn web research) plus a live run of `apps/site` with screenshot review at 1440×900 and 390×844, light/dark emulation. Every finding cites a file path. Findings marked **[verified]** were independently re-checked against source or runtime data.

specs.title: Spacing
specs.summary: cascade uses a fixed spacing scale defined as `--cascivo-space-*` tokens. All spacing in components must reference these tokens rather than hard-coded pixel values.

specs.title: t5-deferral
specs.summary: **Status:** Deferred within v13 **Decision date:** 2026-06-13 **Tranche:** T5 (CSS-native progressive enhancement)

specs.title: trade-dashboard-adjustments
specs.summary: **Status:** Approved plan, ready for implementation **Date:** 2026-07-03 **Scope:** Four adjustments to the shipped `apps/examples/trade` showcase (see `docs/specs/trading-dashboard-showcase.md` for the original spec). Plus two small, opt-in enhancements to shared code that the adjustments require. **Audience:** Implementing agent (Opus). Follow CLAUDE.md rules throughout — signals only, `useSignals()` first in render-reading components, i18n for all strings, canonical breakpoint literals only, `pnpm ready` green before every commit.

specs.title: trading-dashboard-showcase
specs.summary: **Status:** Approved plan, ready for implementation **Date:** 2026-07-03 **Deliverable:** A new example app `apps/examples/trade` (a Trade Republic-style brokerage trading workspace) plus two small, targeted enhancements to `@cascivo/charts`. **Audience:** Implementing agent (Opus/Sonnet). Follow CLAUDE.md rules throughout — signals only (no `useState`/`useEffect`), `useSignals()` first in every render-reading React component, i18n for every user-visible string, mobile-first CSS on the canonical breakpoint scale, `pnpm ready` green before every commit.

specs.title: wcag-2.2
specs.summary: Three criteria added in WCAG 2.2 require explicit cascade handling. This document records the implementation approach and verification status for each.
